parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces 6600+ lines across 84 files including a retry/backoff system, BigModel error dispatch, job tracking, and state management. Several material risks stand out: (1) the retry module has unbounded retry-paths for non-transient errors that could silently burn API quota or loop on persistent failures, (2) BigModel error classification silently falls through to generic handling for error shapes not covered in the dispatch table, (3) tracked-jobs/state mutations lack concurrency guards making them vulnerable to race conditions on parallel invocations, and (4) the `process.mjs` deletion (67 lines removed) with no clear migration path risks latent import failures if any path still references it.

findings.title: retry.mjs retries non-transient errors — silent quota burn or infinite-feel loops
findings.body: The retry module (203 new lines) applies retry logic to API calls, but the classification of which errors are retryable vs permanent is delegated to bigmodel-errors.mjs. If the error dispatcher returns retryable=true for errors that are actually permanent (e.g., 401 auth failures, 403 permission denied, malformed request bodies), the retry loop will repeatedly hit the API with the same bad request, consuming quota and producing no useful result. The tests (retry.test.mjs, 352 lines) may cover happy-path retry but the adversarial concern is the gap: errors that LOOK transient but aren't, or new error codes from the provider that fall into an unclassified bucket and default to retryable. This is a real cost and latency risk in production.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit allowlist of retryable HTTP status codes / error codes in the retry logic. Default to NOT retrying for any error not explicitly matched. Add a hard ceiling on total retry duration wall-clock time (not just count) to bound cost. Add test cases for 401, 403, 400 being correctly classified as non-retryable.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs dispatch table has silent fallthrough for unrecognized error shapes
findings.body: The new BigModel error dispatch module (168 lines) maps known error patterns to retry/recovery strategies. For any error that doesn't match a known pattern, the code appears to fall through to a generic handler. This means: (a) new error types introduced by the upstream API provider will be silently misclassified, (b) there's no logging or alerting when an unclassified error is encountered, making it invisible in production, (c) the caller may take inappropriate recovery action based on a wrong classification. The gap is specifically that there's no 'unknown error' sentinel that triggers conservative behavior (fail-fast) instead of optimistic behavior (retry).
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit default/catch-all branch in the error classifier that logs a warning with the full error shape and returns retryable=false. Add a test that verifies unrecognised error structures are classified as non-retryable. Consider adding a metric/counter for 'unclassified errors' to surface provider API changes.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs and state.mjs lack concurrency safety for parallel invocations
findings.body: The tracked-jobs module (78 lines changed) and state module (32 lines changed) manage job lifecycle state in what appears to be filesystem-backed storage. If two glm-client or companion processes run concurrently (e.g., user triggers two commands in quick succession), both can read-modify-write the same state file, leading to lost updates, corrupted JSON, or orphaned job entries. There's no file locking, atomic writes, or optimistic concurrency control visible in the diff. The race window is real because the retry/backoff system introduces async delays that widen the window for concurrent access.
findings.recommendation: Use atomic file writes (write to temp file + rename) for state updates. Add a simple file lock (e.g., lockfile with pid + timeout) around read-modify-write sequences. At minimum, wrap state mutations in try/catch that detects and reports concurrent modification rather than silently corrupting.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted but residual import risk not validated
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines deleted) was removed entirely. If any file in the codebase still imports from this module — whether via dynamic import, a cached require, or a path not caught by check-imports.mjs — it will throw a runtime MODULE_NOT_FOUND error. The check-imports.mjs script (5 lines changed) may not cover all import patterns (dynamic imports, string-concatenated paths, conditional imports). This is a latent runtime bomb that would only surface when the specific code path is exercised.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for 'process.mjs' or 'lib/process' in any form (dynamic imports, string references). Add a CI check that validates every import in every .mjs file resolves. Consider keeping process.mjs as a re-export shim that throws a deprecation warning instead of deleting it outright, to provide a graceful migration path.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs retry integration may double-report failures to callers
findings.body: glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) now integrates retry logic from retry.mjs. If a request fails after exhausting retries, the error that propagates to the caller may be the last retry's error rather than the original error, losing the root cause. Additionally, if the caller has its own error handling/retry logic (e.g., in glm-companion.mjs which has 139 lines changed), there's a risk of double-retry: the client retries internally, then the caller retries the whole operation, compounding the number of API calls exponentially.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the error propagated after retry exhaustion includes the original error as a cause chain. Document whether callers should or should not add their own retry layer. Add a guard in glm-companion.mjs to skip its own retry if glm-client already retried, or make the retry in glm-client opt-in via a parameter.

next_steps: Read the full content of retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs to verify the retryable classification logic and confirm whether the default is retry or fail-fast
Read tracked-jobs.mjs and state.mjs to confirm whether file-based state has any concurrency protection
Grep for any remaining references to process.mjs across the codebase
Read glm-client.mjs to trace the retry integration and verify error propagation preserves root cause
Verify check-imports.mjs covers dynamic import patterns for the process.mjs deletion safety check
